description: A roll of paper or parchment for writing; document that unfurls to reveal. 'Roela' names what unrolls wisdom—teaching revealed through patient unfolding.
sound_symbolism: The word unrolls. 'Ro' turns in rolling motion, scroll opening. The vowel 'e' opens to revelation. Then 'la' extends long, text continuing.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'roela' (the scroll). More formal than 'pelua' (paper)—implies teaching, history, sacred text.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Teaching unfolds—understanding comes gradually, like a scroll revealing line by line.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Scrolls held wisdom—Torah, sutras, classics, knowledge preserved in rolled form.
